title: a null value is not treated as zero in hash partition table
description:
got:
mysql> select * from th partition(p0);
+------+-------+
| c1   | c2    |
+------+-------+
|    0 | gigan |
+------+-------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)

mysql> select * from th partition(p3);
+------+--------+
| c1   | c2     |
+------+--------+
| NULL | mothra |
+------+--------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)

expect:

mysql> select * from th partition(p0);
+------+--------+
| c1   | c2     |
+------+--------+
|    0 | gigan  |
| NULL | mothra |
+------+--------+
2 rows in set (0.01 sec)

mysql> select * from th partition(p3);
Empty set (0.00 sec)
How to repeat:
create table th(c1 int, c2 varchar(20)) partition by hash(c1) partitions 5;
INSERT INTO th VALUES (NULL, 'mothra'), (0, 'gigan');
select * from th partition(p0);
select * from th partition(p3);
poc: create table th(c1 int, c2 varchar(20)) partition by hash(c1) partitions 5;
key_bug_features: NULL value not treated as zero in hash partition
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE th(c1 INT, c2 VARCHAR(20), c3 DECIMAL(10,2)) PARTITION BY HASH(c1) PARTITIONS 5;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM th WHERE c1 IS NULL;
